commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1e220e6962ebe7d562005695261f98b94b0279fa)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a pre-sequential Telegram callback acknowledgement path for selected final reply-button payloads, sends a visible post-authorization acknowledgement, updates callback tests to use the shared env helper, and bumps one raw-fetch allowlist line.

PR surface: Source +66, Tests +157, Other 0. Total +223 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main installs Telegram sequentialization before the callback handler, so answerCallbackQuery cannot run until the per-chat queue releases. I did not establish a fresh live Telegram reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor or maintainer repair plus exact-head Telegram proof; automation cannot supply the contributor's live proof gate for this external PR.

target.title: telegram: fast-ack final button callbacks
target.body:
## Summary
- replace the Telegram test file's ad-hoc env mutation helper with the shared plugin-sdk withEnvAsync helper so OpenGrep no longer flags raw process.env writes
- update the temporary qqbot raw-fetch allowlist line number from 211 to 224 so check-additional-boundaries-bcd reflects current baseline instead of failing on drift

## Verification
- direct diff inspection of both changed files
- local pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts --reporter=dot was blocked in this fresh checkout by pnpm registry metadata timeouts before dependencies were available
- local node scripts/check-no-raw-channel-fetch.mjs was blocked before dependency install because typescript was not yet present in node_modules

## Context
- supersedes #86162 with the same Telegram fast-ack behavior plus the CI unblocker for the stale raw-fetch allowlist entry
target.author: piersonr

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
